ts,uihVh iksVZ xsV ifjljksa ij jk"Vªh; jktekxZ dh lekfIr ij iz;ksDrk”kqYd laxzghr djus gsrq fj;k;rxzkgh eSllZ ,etsihvkjlh,y dks vuqefr iznku djrh gSA [Qk- la- vkjMCyw@,u,p&37011@7@2005&ihvkblh] nf{krk nkl] la;qDr lfpo MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 11th August, 2016 S.O. 2696(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways (Collection of Fees by any person for the use of section of National Highways/permanent bridge/temporary bridge on National Highway) Rules, 1997, the Central Government by notification of the Government of India vide S.O. No. 1011(E), dated 13.07.2005 authorized the Concessionaire, M/s Mumbai-JNPT Port Road Company Ltd., to collect and retain fees from the mechanical vehicles for the use of section from km. 5.000 to km. 26.987 (A1 to E Section), from km 0.000 to km 4.400 (D-G Section) of National Highway No. 4B (new NH-348) and from km 106.000 to km 109.500 of National Highway No. 4 (new NH-48) in the State of Maharashtra. The fee is to be collected at two plazas, one at km 13.050 and another at km 23.250. And, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-sections (1-A), (1-B), (1-C), (1-D) and (1-E) of section 20 of the Bombay Motor Vehicles Tax Act, 1958 (Bom. LXV of 1958), the Government of Maharashtra by notification no. PSP.2003/CR-249/Road-9A dated 20.11.2010 authorized the Concessionaire, M/s Mumbai-JNPT Port Road Company Ltd., to collect and retain fees from the mechanical vehicles for the use of section from km. 6.400 to 14.550 and km. 0.000 to 6.202 of Amra Marg of SH-54 including construction of new Six Lane Bridge on Panvel creek in the State of Maharashtra. The fee is to be collected at km. 9.100. And, the said section from km. 6.400 to 14.550 and km. 0.000 to 6.202 of Amra Marg of SH-54 has been declared as National Highway no. 348A vide S.O. No. 1513(E), dated 13.06.2014. Now, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways (Collection of Fees by any person for the use of section of National Highways/permanent bridge/temporary bridge on National Highway) Rules, 1997, in partial modification to the Notification S.O. No.1011(E), dated 13.07.2005 published in the Gazette of India , Extraordinary, Part-II, Section 3, Sub-section (ii) of Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry of Shipping, Road Transport and Highways (Department of Road Transport and Highways) and Notification No. PSP.2003/CR-249/Road-9A dated 20.11.2010 published in Gazette of Maharashtra State Government in Public Works Department, Mumbai, Central Government hereby discontinue the collection of user fee at km. 13.050, km. 23.250 and km 9.100 and instead allow Concessionaire M/s MJPRCL to collect the user fee at the end of National Highway at JNPT Port gate complexes. [F. No. RW/NH-37011/7/2005-PIC] DAKSHITA DAS, Jt. Secy.
